title: centaurea trichocephala subsp. simonkaiana
intro: Centaurea trichocephala subsp. simonkaiana, also known as Simonka's knapweed, is a plant species in the dandelion-like family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Simonka's knapweed has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
